Gets Argued

Meet Geo Forum.

Explore the forum

Geo Forum is a structured home for geopolitics — organized by region and topic instead of scattered across a hundred unrelated subreddits.

Reputation that means something

Earn standing through well-received posts and comments — badges like Analyst, Researcher, and Verified Journalist mark the voices worth following.

News, plus
your analysis.

Share a source link and add your own take, instead of just reposting a headline with no context.

Built for
long-form too

Write evergreen, Medium-style analysis with charts, maps, and references — not just hot takes.

What You Can Expect
From Us

About Geo Forum

Geo Forum is a community built for people who want to understand the world beyond headlines. We bring together students, researchers, professionals, and curious minds to discuss geopolitics, international relations, global economics, defense, diplomacy, technology, and world affairs.

Our goal is to create a space where informed discussions, diverse perspectives, and evidence-based analysis take priority over misinformation and sensationalism. Whether you're following breaking international events, exploring historical context, or debating the future of global power, Geo Forum provides a platform to learn, share, and engage.

We encourage respectful conversations and thoughtful debate. Members are welcome to post news, analysis, research, questions, maps, data visualizations, and opinions, provided they contribute constructively to the discussion.

Our Mission

To make geopolitics more accessible by fostering an open community where people can exchange ideas, deepen their understanding of global affairs, and stay informed about events shaping the world.

What You'll Find

  • ✦Breaking geopolitical news and updates
  • ✦In-depth analysis of international events
  • ✦Discussions on diplomacy, defense, and security
  • ✦Global economic and trade insights
  • ✦Regional and country-specific conversations
  • ✦Historical context behind current events
  • ✦Community-driven questions, debates, and research
  • ✦Maps, statistics, and educational resources

Our Community Values

  • ✦Respect different viewpoints.
  • ✦Support claims with reliable sources whenever possible.
  • ✦Engage in civil and constructive discussions.
  • ✦Avoid misinformation, hate speech, and personal attacks.
  • ✦Focus on learning, critical thinking, and meaningful dialogue.

Geo Forum is independent and community-driven. We believe understanding global affairs requires curiosity, critical thinking, and respectful discussion. Whether you're new to geopolitics or have years of experience studying international relations, there's a place for you here.

Use Cases

File No. 002 — Geo Forum

Geo Forum is designed for anyone who wants to stay informed, learn, and engage in meaningful discussions about global affairs. Whether you're a student, researcher, professional, or simply curious about the world, the platform offers a space to explore different perspectives and share knowledge.

01

Stay Updated

Follow discussions on international events, diplomatic developments, conflicts, elections, economic trends, and other major geopolitical stories as they unfold.

02

Participate in Discussions

Share your opinions, ask questions, and engage in respectful conversations with members from different backgrounds and viewpoints.

03

Publish Analysis

Write detailed analyses, explain complex geopolitical topics, and contribute original insights backed by reliable sources and data.

04

Research and Learning

Explore historical events, international relations, military strategy, global economics, and political systems through community-driven discussions and educational content.

05

Share News

Post news articles from credible sources and discuss their implications with the community while providing context and multiple viewpoints.

06

Regional Communities

Join discussions focused on specific countries, regions, or international organizations to better understand local and global developments.

07

Data and Visualizations

Share maps, charts, infographics, timelines, and statistics that help explain geopolitical events and trends.

08

Ask Questions

Seek explanations about international conflicts, treaties, organizations, historical events, or current affairs from knowledgeable community members.

09

Follow Topics

Discover and follow topics that interest you, including diplomacy, defense, cybersecurity, energy, trade, climate policy, international law, and emerging technologies.

10

Build Your Profile

Create your profile, publish posts, participate in discussions, receive feedback from the community, and build a reputation through meaningful contributions.

11

Save and Revisit

Bookmark important discussions, research posts, and articles for future reference.

12

Community Moderation

Help maintain a respectful and informative environment by reporting inappropriate content, supporting fact-based discussions, and following the community guidelines.

Who Can
Benefit

  • 01Students studying political science, international relations, history, economics, or law.
  • 02Researchers and academics exploring global affairs.
  • 03Journalists and analysts tracking international developments.
  • 04Professionals working in government, policy, defense, or global business.
  • 05Content creators, educators, and writers.
  • 06Anyone interested in understanding world events through informed discussion.

Geo Forum is built to encourage thoughtful conversations, evidence-based analysis, and respectful debate, helping members better understand the complex issues shaping our world.
